authoritative_sources.title: Customer Identification Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC examination-manual content used by federal banking regulators to assess customer-identification, verification, notice, and recordkeeping controls at onboarding.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Identity intake, verification, and recordkeeping validation
used_for: hard-stop conditions for incomplete identity evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Customer Due Diligence
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC guidance defining customer due diligence, understanding customer relationships, and risk-based control expectations that shape onboarding readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk understanding and customer profile assembly
used_for: boundary between onboarding checks and downstream monitoring

authoritative_sources.title: Customer Due Diligence Requirements for Financial Institutions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary FinCEN rule and guidance hub for CDD and beneficial-ownership handling, including the current February 13, 2026 relief posture that affects repeat collection assumptions.
used_for: current-source conflict handling for repeat collection

authoritative_sources.title: Anti-Money Laundering Program and Suspicious Activity Reporting Requirements for Insurance Companies Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN FAQ that makes the lane specific to the financial-services-and-insurance profile by clarifying AML-program and suspicious-activity expectations for covered insurance products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Insurance-product AML boundary handling and escalation
used_for: adjacent-lane routing for covered insurance products

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary OFAC guidance describing sanctions-compliance program expectations, risk assessment, controls, testing, and training that determine onboarding stop conditions when sanctions hits appear.

authoritative_sources.title: Supplementing Fraud Detection Standards for WEB Debits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ACH operating-rules source for account-validation and fraud-detection expectations that are directly relevant when onboarding includes payment-account setup and consumer-impact risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payments account validation and fraud-control gating

authoritative_sources.title: Digital Identity Guidelines: Identity Proofing and Enrollment (SP 800-63A)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public identity-proofing and enrollment standard that defines evidence strength, identity resolution, and enrollment controls applicable to digital financial-services onboarding.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Identity proofing and enrollment control design

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized financial-services terminology standard used to normalize party, account, payment, and exception artifacts across KYC, onboarding, payments, and servicing systems.

workflow_stages.name: Onboarding intake and subject grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is tied to the correct application, account, policy, customer, legal entity, jurisdiction, and product context before any readiness claim is made.

workflow_stages.name: Identity, documentary, and non-documentary verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate identity evidence, notice delivery, and recordkeeping requirements before treating the subject as sufficiently identified for onboarding progression.

workflow_stages.name: Due diligence, beneficial-ownership, and risk understanding
workflow_stages.description: Check customer profile, legal-entity ownership or control information, and risk-based due-diligence artifacts needed to continue safely.

workflow_stages.name: Sanctions, fraud, AML, and consumer-impact stop review
workflow_stages.description: Escalate OFAC hits, suspicious funding patterns, insurance AML issues, or fraud and consumer-impact signals rather than resolving them informally in-lane.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Setup, permissions, and go-live readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate access grants, training, account validation, onboarding work items, and approval-chain completion before handoff into steady-state operations.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Traceable handoff and exception packaging
workflow_stages.description: Produce evidence-backed onboarding checklists, readiness summaries, handoff notes, and escalation packets that preserve unresolved blockers and the correct next owner.

artifacts: application, account, policy, and party profile records
CIP notice logs and documentary or non-documentary verification evidence
CDD questionnaires, customer risk-profile records, and onboarding case notes
beneficial-owner or control-person certifications and legal-entity supporting documents
sanctions-screening, fraud-monitoring, AML-review, and approval-history artifacts
consumer disclosures, privacy notices, and customer communication logs
IAM tickets, role grants, training completion records, and setup work items
payment-account validation, policy-admin setup, and handoff or deferred-item records
systems_of_record: fraud monitoring, sanctions-screening, and AML review systems
general ledger, reconciliation, or payment-network reference systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate KYC, disclosure, access, training, and setup evidence needed for onboarding readiness.
Prepare onboarding checklists, readiness summaries, handoff notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership changes.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, suspicious-activity, consumer-protection, or policy-interpretation issues materially affect the safe answer.
Identity, beneficial-ownership, disclosure, or system-of-record conflicts cannot be resolved from the retrieved source set.
refuse: Mark onboarding complete or go-live ready without evidence-backed prerequisite closure.
Approve legal interpretation, sanctions clearance, underwriting decisions, or policy overrides outside the lane.
Fabricate verification, beneficial-ownership, approval, disclosure, or regulated-advice conclusions.

domain_failure_modes: Treating partial identity evidence as sufficient and activating a customer before documentary, non-documentary, or notice requirements are complete.
Waiving beneficial-owner or control-person evidence for speed even though the legal-entity record is incomplete or contested.
Collapsing OFAC, fraud, AML, or suspicious-funding signals into generic onboarding friction instead of escalating them.
Accepting broader system permissions than approved and calling setup complete despite access-control mismatches.
Treating payment-account validation as a substitute for CIP, CDD, or sanctions controls.
Using stale or contradictory onboarding, policy-admin, payments, or document records to assert readiness as final truth.
Overgeneralizing KYC onboarding into generic customer onboarding language that omits CIP, CDD, beneficial-ownership, sanctions, and disclosure boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-specific onboarding rules vary across depository, payments, brokerage, and covered-insurance contexts, especially when a tenant applies stricter beneficial-ownership, enhanced-due-diligence, or disclosure controls than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require product, jurisdiction, and tenant-policy retrieval at runtime and escalate when those differences materially change readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Current FinCEN relief may narrow when repeat beneficial-ownership collection is required, but tenant policy or adjacent compliance owners may still require refreshed certifications for risk reasons.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Record the public baseline, preserve the tenant overlay, and escalate rather than assuming relief eliminates stricter tenant controls.
